Landing.nosc-is-description: is a virtual digital innovation hub that provides digital innovation services for researchers, the business sector, and public authorities using cloud technologies and other advanced digital technologies.
Landing.nosc-purpose-title: The purpose of NOSC-UA DIH is to promote
Landing.nosc-purpose-description-1: cooperation between research infrastructures and innovative companies (including SMEs) based on digital technologies
Landing.nosc-purpose-description-2: the development of new products and services based on green and digital technologies, involving the innovation potential of scientific institutions of the National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine
Landing.nosc-purpose-description-3: the integration of Ukrainian SMEs and researchers into the European Research Area and the EU Digital Single Market.
Landing.feature-1: Joint development and piloting of digital innovation projects
Landing.feature-3: Access to an innovation ecosystem and networking
Landing.feature-4: Development of digital skills through education
Landing.industries-title: Areas of activity of the Digital Innovation Hub
Landing.industries-4: Digital technologies centre for non-destructive testing
Landing.partners-description: The infrastructure base for the Virtual Centre for Digital Science is the cloud cluster of the Ukrainian National Grid and the educational-research platform of the KAU Data Science Research Center. The implementation of the main project tasks focuses on the development of the UNG cloud grid infrastructure.

AboutUs.paragraph-1: Within the framework of the project of the Kyiv Academic University of the National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine and the Ministry of Education and Science of Ukraine (KAU) "Development of the Virtual Centre of Digital Science" under the targeted comprehensive programme of scientific research of the National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine "Grid infrastructure and grid technologies for scientific and applied research", during 2018–2019 a web catalogue of cloud services of the Virtual Centre of Digital Science (VCDS) was created on the basis of the cloud cluster of the Ukrainian National Grid.
AboutUs.paragraph-2: The cloud cluster enables efficient use of computing resources by implementing the "on-demand resource allocation" model. Currently, the UA-BITP cloud site is the only cloud resource in Ukraine that meets all EGI requirements for availability and reliability and is included in the EGI Federated Cloud resources.
AboutUs.paragraph-3: In 2020, the Bogolyubov Institute for Theoretical Physics of the National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine (ITP), which was authorised by the Government of Ukraine (letter of the Ministry of Education and Science of Ukraine No. 1/11-6930 dated 09.10.2020) to represent Ukraine in the EOSC Association, submitted an application for membership in the EOSC Association. On 21 October 2020, an ITP representative took part in the EOSC Symposium, where draft versions of key EOSC documents, including the EOSC Association Statutes, were presented.
AboutUs.paragraph-4: On 17 December 2020, during the meeting of the General Assembly (EOSC General Assembly), the Bogolyubov Institute for Theoretical Physics of the National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine was recognised as a full member of the EOSC Association as the official representative of Ukraine. The EOSC Association unites 142 members, including 21 members with a national mandate (including Ukraine) and 49 members with observer status. The members of the EOSC Association include leading European research organisations, resource centres, and providers of grid and cloud services actively involved in the implementation of the European Open Science Cloud concept.

UNG.title: Support for researches
UNG.intro: EGI's support community is organised at country level. National e-Infrastructure (NGI) in Ukraine – Ukrainian National Grid, offers support to local researchers services in the following areas:
UNG.ung-intro: The Ukrainian National Grid (UNG) is an e-infrastructure set up to share and manage the resources of 26 Ukrainian institutes and universities. The UNG has 14 clusters fully integrated in the EGI Federation since June 2012. The Ukrainian resources are available to researchers through 9 Virtual Organisations (VOs):
UNG.medical-grid-system: Medical grid-system for research in the field of cardiology
UNG.solution-wide-range: Solution of a wide range of scientific and applied problems
UNG.calculation-of-three-dimensional: Calculation of three-dimensional seismic full-wave models in a grid environment
UNG.partnership-ung: Ukraine has joined the EGI Council in 2018 as an Associated Participant after a fruitful collaboration going back for many years.
UNG.founding: Ukrainian National Grid was founded as the targeted state scientific and technological project (program) on the development and implementation of grid-technologies adopted by the Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine in 2009.

EGI.intro: EGI delivers advanced computing services to support scientists, multinational projects and research infrastructures. The EGI Services are provided by EGI's federated cloud providers and data centres.
EGI.body-1-pre: The services can be requested by everyone involved in academic research and businesses via the
EGI.body-2-pre: Examples of how researchers use our services can be found in the
EGI.body-3: The EGI Federated Cloud is a IaaS-type cloud, made of academic private clouds and virtualised resources and built around open standards. Its development is driven by requirements of the scientific community. The result is a new type of research e-infrastructure, based on the mature federated operations services that make EGI a reliable resource for science. When using EGI Federated Cloud resources, researchers and research communities can count on:
EGI.feature-2: elastic resource consumption based on real need
EGI.feature-3: immediately processed workloads – no more waiting time
EGI.feature-4: an extended e-infrastructure across resource providers in Europe
EGI.feature-5: service performance scaled with elastic resource consumption
EGI.body-4: The EGI Federation is an international e-infrastructure set up to provide advanced computing and data analytics services for research and innovation. The EGI e-infrastructure is publicly-funded and comprises hundreds of data centres and cloud providers spread across Europe and worldwide.
EGI.federation-bullet-1-pre: The EGI Federation offers a wide range of services for compute, storage, data and support.
EGI.federation-bullet-2-pre: The EGI Federation provides access to +1,000,000 computing cores and +740 PB of disk and tape storage.
EGI.final: Ukraine has joined the EGI Council in 2018 as an Associated Participant after a fruitful collaboration going back for many years.

EOSC.intro-1: EOSC Portal serves as a universal entry point to the EOSC services, offering distributed and cloud computing resources enabling researchers and other users to process and analyse data in a distributed computing environment, access and order public and commercial e-infrastructure services supplied at national, regional and institutional levels.
EOSC.intro-2: It helps to develop synergies and complementarity in data handling between research infrastructures, optimise technological implementation thus contributing to the development of a consistent European research infrastructures ecosystem and ensure integration and interoperability of data and tools within the EOSC.
EOSC.intro-3: From the supplier side, it offers local, national and international providers the opportunity to advertise their own services and resources, which is compliant with the needs of European research community. Visit the Catalogue & Marketplace or choose from the categories below.
EOSC.body-1: EU countries and countries associated with Horizon 2020, represented in the EOSC Governance Board, agreed unanimously to run the EOSC as a co-programmed European Partnership under Horizon Europe from 2021.
EOSC.body-2: The proposal for a candidate EOSC partnership was published following a process of co-creating its vision including strategic and operational objectives to be achieved by 2027.
EOSC.body-3: In July 2020, an EOSC Association was set up to provide a single voice for advocacy and represent the broader EOSC stakeholder community. This association aims to become operational by early 2021 and rapidly expand its membership.
EOSC.body-4: The new governance model agreed with EU countries for the next EOSC implementation phase after 2020 will be tripartite including:
EOSC.governance-1: The EU represented by the Commission
EOSC.governance-2-pre: The European research community represented by the
EOSC.governance-3: EU countries and countries associated with Horizon 2020 represented through a Steering Board to be set up in 2021 outside of the EOSC Association
EOSC.final: Bogolyubov Institute for Theoretical Physics of the National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine officially represents Ukraine in EOSC Association

ScienceServices.title: Digital Science Services
ScienceServices.article-1-description: The Numerical Weather Prediction service for regional weather forecasting allows users to submit calculation requests for a specific region and receive forecasts on Google Maps. The computing service is implemented based on numerical methods and parallel computing models for solving meteorological forecasting problems...
ScienceServices.article-2-description: Introduction Integrated Hydro-Kinetic Model (iHKM) is the powerful tool for simulation of a relativistic heavy ion collision, which allows to describe or predict a wide variety of measured bulk observables. Naturally, a researcher is interested in finding the connection between...

InnovationCenter.title: Digital Innovation Centre
InnovationCenter.description: The Virtual Digital Innovation Centre of the Kyiv Academic University (NOSC-UA DIH) provides digital innovation services for researchers, the business sector, and public authorities using cloud technologies and other advanced digital technologies:
InnovationCenter.generalModel: According to the general operating model of digital innovation hubs, NOSC-UA DIH develops an innovation ecosystem and provides its participants with basic services for the implementation of digital technologies:
InnovationCenter.technologies-1: integration, adaptation and configuration of various technologies
InnovationCenter.purpose: The purpose of NOSC-UA DIH is to promote:
InnovationCenter.purpose-1: cooperation between research infrastructures and innovative companies (including SMEs) based on digital technologies;
InnovationCenter.purpose-2: development of new products and services based on ‘green’ and digital technologies with the involvement of the innovative potential of scientific institutions of the National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine;
InnovationCenter.purpose-3: integration of Ukrainian SMEs and researchers into the European Research Area and the EU Digital Single Market.
InnovationCenter.description-1: NOSC-UA DIH was launched in 2021 as a non-profit project coordinated by the Kyiv Academic University (KAU) within the framework of the Targeted Programme of Scientific Research of the National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine (NASU) “Mathematical Modelling in Interdisciplinary Research of Processes and Systems Based on Intelligent Supercomputing, Grid and Cloud Technologies” for 2021–2025.
InnovationCenter.description-2: In January 2024, NOSC-UA DIH signed an agreement together with another Kyiv digital innovation hub “Centre 4.0 Igor Sikorsky Kyiv Polytechnic Institute” (Centre 4.0 KPI DIH) and 12 regional partners to establish the “Kyiv HighTech” DIH, coordinated by the Ukrainian Cluster Alliance. In July, within the Digital Europe Programme call, the “Kyiv HighTech” DIH received international status as a European Digital Innovation Hub (EDIH) from the European Commission.

InnovationCenterPartners.title: Digital Innovation Centre
InnovationCenterPartners.ecosystem-participant-1: The National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine (NASU) and its 12 research institutes representing the Academy.City Research Cooperation Association
InnovationCenterPartners.ecosystem-participant-2: Kyiv City State Administration – KAU’s partner in establishing the Academic.City Science Park
InnovationCenterPartners.dih-network-2: Digital Innovation Hub of Moldova (DIH, Moldova)
InnovationCenterPartners.dih-network-5: Hub-laboratory Internet of Things: DIH I4MS (Ukraine)
InnovationCenterPartners.enterprise-2: LLC “Scientific Park of Kyiv National Economic University (KNEU)”

InnovationCenterServices.title: Our Services
InnovationCenterServices.description: NOSC-UA DIH offers a wide range of services to support the digital transformation of your business
InnovationCenterServices.form-notice: Submit your application for NOSC-UA DIH services using the form
InnovationCenterServices.service-1-title: Co-development and Piloting of Digital Innovation Projects
InnovationCenterServices.service-1-description:
• Strategic support for research, development and innovation (R&D&I)

• Contract research (e.g. specific R&D, Technology concept development / Proof of Concept)

• Technical support for project scaling (e.g. defining functional requirements for digital products, proof of concept, prototyping)

• Infrastructure provision (e.g. support in using cloud technology infrastructure, services, and platforms (i4Trust, FIWARE, iShare, DataverseUA, EOSC, EGI)
InnovationCenterServices.service-2-title: Investment Support and Fundraising, Grants for Digital Innovation Development
InnovationCenterServices.service-2-description:
• Individual and group fundraising consultations

• Thematic seminars, webinars and trainings

• Grant competition matching for clients

• Support for grant application submission and execution

• Business strategy and development consulting

• Legal support on intellectual property and technology transfer
InnovationCenterServices.service-3-title: Access to the Innovation Ecosystem and Networking
InnovationCenterServices.service-3-description:
• Support and development of digital innovation ecosystems. Support at the stage of launching a digital initiative and forming a community development strategy (Innovation networks, clusters, technology platforms, civic associations, business initiatives)

• Ecosystem events for SMEs (informational, educational and networking webinars, technology workshops, training courses. Organizing Innovation Days, brokerage events, networking sessions to initiate collaboration between businesses and research groups based on modern digital technologies)

• Industry 5.0 development and digital ecosystem events for the Kyiv region in collaboration with regional partners and authorities

• Technology scouting, ecosystem attraction and management (e.g. trend monitoring, technology and third-party research, ecosystem mapping, ecosystem management/orchestration, networking, matchmaking)

• Support for access to international digital ecosystems and their services (EOSC, FIWARE, DIH World, EDIHs, i4Trust, iShare, Dataverse, EGI)
InnovationCenterServices.service-4-description:
• Organizing educational events (webinars, schools, workshops)

• Training sessions (online and offline)

• Access to training courses and learning platforms

• Technology consultations (supported by platforms: FIWARE, EOSC, EGI, Dataverse)
InnovationCenterServices.service-5-description:
• Data governance maturity assessment

• Data strategy development: alignment with business strategy; data use cases; data operating model

• Enterprise data management: data location, data quality and security, analytics and statistical data

• Conducting data analysis
InnovationCenterServices.service-6-title: Data Sharing and Processing, Data Spaces
InnovationCenterServices.service-6-description:
• Application of data space and digital twin construction technologies

• Technical consulting in the creation, joining, integration and operation of data spaces for secure and efficient management, integration and sharing of valuable data

• Use of data space construction technologies, including i4Trust requirements, FIWARE building blocks and security policies, as well as digital twins based on Smart Data Models

• Data interoperability solutions enabling machines and systems to seamlessly exchange information in your industrial environment

• Enhanced development and management of Cyber-Physical Industrial Systems (CPIS) through integration of Model-Based Engineering (MBE), Digital Twins and DevOps practices

• Data-driven business models for generating new revenue streams and improving operational efficiency
InnovationCenterServices.service-7-description:
• Adoption of cloud technologies, architecture design and cloud infrastructure configuration

• Providing access to the NOSC-UA cloud platform for research and innovation requiring big data processing, cloud computing and simulation

• Technical support for access to cloud resources: virtual servers, resource monitoring, ready-to-use runtime environments and applications — SaaS, data storage for virtual environments, cloud computing, HPC

• Assistance with accessing cloud resources from EOSC ecosystem providers

• Access to advanced EGI computing services
InnovationCenterServices.service-8-description:
• Consulting on AI/ML technology adoption, problem formulation, tool and method selection

• Case studies and model testing. Dataset exploration, data preprocessing and feature engineering, data mining (training, testing and cross-validation of AI&ML models)

• Creation, integration and operation of AI/ML-based solutions

• Cloud computing and simulation based on artificial intelligence technologies, cloud service for Deep Learning task computation

• Development of automated systems for building and updating predictive models for a given problem

• AI&ML application development
InnovationCenterServices.service-9-description:
Blockchain Consulting is a service that helps companies integrate blockchain technology into their business. Blockchain consultants can help companies:

• Determine whether blockchain is suitable for the client's needs

• Develop a blockchain implementation strategy

• Select the appropriate blockchain platform

• Implement blockchain solutions

• Integrate blockchain with existing systems

• Train staff to work with blockchain

• Expanding educational opportunities — comprehensive training on blockchain fundamentals and applications

• Strategic design thinking — dedicated sessions to explore blockchain capabilities and map integration strategies

• Idea validation and optimization — rigorous assessment of concepts for viability and optimization recommendations
InnovationCenterServices.service-10-description:
• Cyber defense organization for information systems

Development and implementation of cyber defense systems in accordance with global best practices. Risk analysis, secure architecture design, access policy configuration and control mechanisms

• Incident response and recovery after attacks

Rapid incident response: malware detection, breach remediation, forensics, system integrity restoration and protection against re-compromise

• Reverse engineering of mobile applications (iOS/Android)

In-depth analysis of mobile applications to identify backdoors, malicious components, potentially dangerous logic and embedded third-party libraries

• Penetration testing (pentest)

Simulation of real-world attacks to assess the security level of web resources, APIs and internal infrastructure. Includes Black Box, Grey Box and White Box scenarios. Detailed reports with recommendations provided

• Round-the-clock monitoring and active protection (24/7)

Continuous monitoring, real-time threat detection and automated protection of web resources and critical systems. Implemented using our proprietary Dataguard product, built on Cloudflare technologies

• Source code and smart contract auditing

Comprehensive review of software code and blockchain solutions. Identification of vulnerabilities, logic errors and non-compliance with security standards

• Staff training and attack simulations

Organizing internal training sessions, phishing campaigns, Red Team / Blue Team scenarios to raise employee awareness and readiness against cyber threats
InnovationCenterServices.service-11-description:
• Manufacturing/design of technological equipment and their components

• 2D and 3D modeling

• 3D printing using FDM and SLA technologies

• Mechanical processing (turning, milling, grinding)

• Metal rolling, strip and foil production

• Cold gas dynamic spraying of functional and protective coatings, thermal spray coating

• Vacuum induction alloy melting

• Vacuum annealing

• Air annealing and hardening with heating up to 1300 ℃

• Manufacturing of glassware (including quartz materials) for scientific research needs: laboratory fixtures for chemical and physical research

• Laser cutting and engraving (plywood, plastic (excluding PVC), textiles)
InnovationCenterServices.service-12-description:
• Digitalization of business objectives

• Review of digital channels and social media traffic

• Channel promotion vision

• Communication model

• Tools and technical solutions

• Sub-channel strategies — SEO / PPC / SMM
InnovationCenterServices.service-13-description:
• Forward and reverse engineering

• Development of design documentation per ESKD, EN, ISO, DIN standards

• Outsourced design bureau on a permanent basis

• Creation of 3D models based on scan copies

• Development of interactive catalogs and work instructions for technological processes

• Development of service documentation with the option to apply augmented reality tools
InnovationCenterServices.service-14-title: Assessment and Development of Organizational Digital Maturity
InnovationCenterServices.service-14-description:
• Diagnosis of the current level of digital maturity (assessment of processes, technologies, data, staff competencies and management practices using a defined maturity model)

• Identification of digital gaps and development priorities (identifying weaknesses and areas of greatest impact of digital initiatives on business outcomes)

• Roadmap development (creating a phased digital transformation plan with specific initiatives, timelines, responsibilities and expected outcomes)

• Development of digital competencies and management approaches (team training, updating management processes and data-driven decision-making culture)

• Progress monitoring and re-assessment (regular measurement of changes, plan adjustments and support for continuous improvement of organizational digital maturity)
InnovationCenterServices.service-15-title: Energy Storage and Accumulation Systems at Microgrid Level (up to 1 MWh)
InnovationCenterServices.service-15-description:
• Energy audit

• Data processing and analysis, digital twin development

• Design and installation of power supply systems based on needs analysis (for enterprise own consumption, for market balancing, for surplus energy sales)

• Construction and installation of engineering infrastructure facilities

• Solar power plant construction

SoftwareArchitecture.constructor-title: “Online Dialogue-Based Constructor of Syntactically Correct Programs“ (ODCSCP)
SoftwareArchitecture.intro-1: Self-balancing of a program onto a target platform is carried out based on the concept of 'Growth Points' of productivity in the architecture of various software, applied to increase the speed of parallel computations.
SoftwareArchitecture.intro-2: Development of formal methods and program tools based on the algebraico-algorithmic approach for automating the programming of computing systems for parallel execution, including multiprocessor platforms of various architectures, as well as application of the developed tools when creating and demonstrating practical systems for automation of scientific research, in particular:
SoftwareArchitecture.service-1: development of algorithmic tools and solutions for the transportation of parallel programs across a wide range of multiprocessor platforms (from scalar processors, GPUs and computing clusters), enabling simplified formalised transformation of programs using rewriting rules
SoftwareArchitecture.service-2: development of new tools for parallel algorithms within the framework of algebra of algorithms with a given specification for a class of applied algorithmic programs
SoftwareArchitecture.service-3: construction of formal models for automatic program optimisation based on the hardware capabilities of processor systems
SoftwareArchitecture.service-4: based on the constructed algebraico-algorithmic methods and techniques, the following software toolkits have been developed:
SoftwareArchitecture.tool-1-title: Interactive Program Design Toolkit (LGS) / Dynamic Computer Syntactically Correct Program Designer (DCKP)
SoftwareArchitecture.tool-1-description: Designed for algorithmically selecting algorithms constructed within the CAE framework / program environments for various subject domains (in particular, mathematical modelling).
SoftwareArchitecture.tool-2-description: Automatic synthesis of parallel programs on target platforms, based on program code transformation using rewriting rules.
SoftwareArchitecture.tool-3-description: Designed for detailed modelling of the execution of parallel applications to support heterogeneous computing systems with graphics accelerators.
SoftwareArchitecture.provider-description-1: Research carried out in the Department of Computer Computations Theory at ITP NASU originates from the foundational work of Academician V.M. Glushkov and his successors in the development of Algorithmic Algebra Systems (AAS) and studies in algebra of algorithms on algorithmic platforms based on systems of rewriting rules.
SoftwareArchitecture.provider-description-2: A special mode of algebra of algorithms with formalisation of programming processes and synthesis of computing algorithms and programs of various classes. The proposed method and tools for formal optimisation of programs from the aspects of forming levels suitable for their domains. On this basis, under the supervision of the department head, Dr. Sci., Prof. A.Yu. Doroshenko, algebraico-algorithmic methods and algebraic algorithm methods have been developed, including formalisation of the algebra of algorithms and horizontal discrete mathematics.
SoftwareArchitecture.projects-title: Projects for service development and application
SoftwareArchitecture.project-1: tools have been developed for creating new numerical methods and computational models for studying methodological modelling problems, enabling the improvement of the quality of methodological research
SoftwareArchitecture.project-2: program tools for implementing algorithmic computations for general research of natural science problems, provided to the State Service of Ukraine and NASU of Ukraine

Blockchain.service-1: software architecture development for systems based on blockchain technology
Blockchain.service-2: development of private blockchain networks using HyperLedger Fabric
Blockchain.service-3: consulting for government organisations, energy, ESG and finance sectors on implementing blockchain-based registries
Blockchain.service-4: NFT marketplace for digital art technologies
Blockchain.service-5: consulting on using blockchain platforms for storing cybercrime evidence
Blockchain.experience-1: development of Self Sovereign Identity projects with faculty partner 482.soluitions
Blockchain.experience-2: development of blockchain based Electronic Student Records for universities
Blockchain.experience-3: tokenization project Green bonds exchange for trading of tokenized carbon units
Blockchain.experience-4: crowdinvesting platform Realium.coop for tokenization of startups

Cybersecurity.service-1-description: With our partner A10 Networks we deliver flexible solutions to protect your services, applications and processes from coordinated and massive attacks.
Cybersecurity.service-2-description: Combining intelligent products and services from our partner Panda Security we are able to protect your infrastructure and PCs with other endpoint from malware and viruses, as well as monitor all the processes to enable the fastest response.
Cybersecurity.service-3-description: With EnCase package from Guidance Software we can achieve new level of operational security and deliver instruments of E-Discovery for your Cybersecurity Department to enable continuous analyses and collecting of evidences in compliance with 27k standards.
Cybersecurity.service-4-description: Being partner of MITEL for last 10 years we can create or swap your voice and video communications to make it absolutely secure and trustworthy, either classic voice infrastructure or wireless and unified communications.
Cybersecurity.service-5-description: Together with CA Technologies we offer a solution for controlling users with advanced and administrative privileges in three environments — in traditional corporate data centers, in a virtual environment and in the cloud.
Cybersecurity.service-6-description: We implement Tufin in customer's networks and ecosystems to analyse, optimize and orchestrate policies to enable overall efficiency and transparency of ICT infrastructure for cybersecurity department.
Cybersecurity.provider-description-1: is an official reseller and engineering company of leading products and solutions for cybersecurity and information protection, optimization, visualization and control of applications, analysis and optimization of network policies, behavioral analysis of users, secure communications, as well as data storage systems and DPI solutions from leading world manufacturers in Ukraine.
Cybersecurity.provider-description-2: The company's portfolio includes products of such well-known global manufacturers as: Panda Security, A10 Networks, Tufin, Thales (SafeNet), Ribbon communications (Sonus), Mapu, Infinidat, Mitel, Guidance as well as our own products for Digital Forensics, Threat Intelligence and Resilience.
Cybersecurity.provider-description-3: In a short time, we have built working principles that are based on an individual approach to the needs of each client and which all employees of the company adhere to: focus on long-term, mutually beneficial partnerships; fulfillment of obligations; mutual respect.
Cybersecurity.experience-project-description: RESPONSE is a five-year #HorizonD2020 project that aims to establish a strategic vision for #SmartCities #EnergyTransition. iSolutions is a horizontal cybersecurity partner and develops its solutions to have holistic cybersecurity management in smart cities and positive energy districts.

ArtificialIntelligence.service-1: research of data sets and selection of approaches for modelling, testing and training of a neural network
ArtificialIntelligence.service-3: cloud service for neural network computing
ArtificialIntelligence.service-4: business consulting on machine learning implementation

CloudTechnologies.title: Services
CloudTechnologies.introduction: The EOSC DIH as an international and multi-partner cooperation supports companies in easily accessing the digital technologies and services offered by the EOSC. It combines 4 main pillars to help companies become more competitive:
CloudTechnologies.pillars-1: Pilot design and co-design: where pilots services or products are designed and/or tested.
CloudTechnologies.pillars-2: Training and support: technical consultancy, business coaching, funding search.
CloudTechnologies.pillars-3: Visibility: inclusion of company solutions in the EOSC Marketplace and exploiting the EOSC networking and ecosystem opportunities.
CloudTechnologies.pillars-4: Technical access: HTC, HPC, cloud computing, machine learning, research data, data management services, and several tools and applications.
CloudTechnologies.ukrainianGrid-intro: Ukrainian National Grid, as national grid e-Infrastructure (NGI) in Ukraine offers support to local researchers services in the following areas:
CloudTechnologies.providers-egi-intro: The EGI e-infrastructure is publicly-funded and comprises hundreds of data centres and cloud providers spread across Europe and worldwide.
CloudTechnologies.providers-egi-point-1: the EGI Federation offers a wide range of services for compute, storage, data and support. More about EGI Services.
CloudTechnologies.providers-egi-point-2: the EGI Federation provides access to +1,000,000 computing cores and +740 PB of disk and tape storage. More about the federation.
CloudTechnologies.providers-eosc-intro: The EOSC DIH as an international and multi-partner cooperation supports companies in easily accessing the digital technologies and services offered by the EOSC.
CloudTechnologies.providers-eosc-registered: EOSC DIH is registered in the S3platform of the European Commission.
CloudTechnologies.providers-eosc-project: The EOSC-hub project is responsible for the creation of the EOSC DIH and provided its initial funding. The project mobilises providers from the EGI Federation, EUDAT CDI, INDIGO-DataCloud and other major European research infrastructures to deliver a common catalogue of research data, services and software for research.
CloudTechnologies.providers-eosc-ukrainianGrid: The Ukrainian National Grid (UNG) is an e-infrastructure set up to share and manage the resources of 26 Ukrainian institutes and universities. The UNG has 14 clusters fully integrated in the EGI Federation since June 2012. The Ukrainian resources are available to researchers through 9 Virtual Organisations (VOs):
CloudTechnologies.partnership-ung: UNG has joined the EGI Council in 2018 as an Associated Participant after a fruitful collaboration going back for many years.
CloudTechnologies.medical-grid-system: Medical grid-system for research in the field of cardiology
CloudTechnologies.solution-wide-range: Solution of a wide range of scientific and applied problems
CloudTechnologies.calculation-of-three-dimensional: Calculation of three-dimensional seismic full-wave models in a grid environment
CloudTechnologies.experience-intro: Examples of how researchers use our services can be found in the
